description:
The killer feature. After matching pattern P against a value of type T:
- the env gets bindings whose types reflect what the match constrains
- subsequent clauses see the scrutinee with prior matched values subtracted (T \\ matched_so_far)

Multi-clause functions get typed as the INTERSECTION of arrows, one per clause. fn fact(0)/1 ∩ fn fact(pos_int)/pos_int is the canonical case.

Guard refinement (e.g. is_integer(x) and x > 0 narrows x) goes here.

Deliverable: pattern_to_type(p) -> Descr for the matched values; envelope_after_match(env, p, t) -> (env', remaining_t).

comments.text: Work landed inside fz-ul4.4 (commit e608a1b) per user's decision to collapse 4.4 + 4.5. Pattern narrowing + multi-clause intersection are both in src/typer.rs.

title: Pattern-match narrowing + multi-clause intersection
